title: Distribution handshake protocol — challenge-response authentication
purpose: The distribution handshake authenticates connecting nodes using a shared cookie and challenge-response. Both nodes exchange names, capabilities (distribution flags), and prove knowledge of the cookie via MD5 challenge hashing. This prevents unauthorized nodes from joining the cluster.
task: Implement the BEAM distribution handshake protocol for both outbound (initiator) and inbound (responder) sides.

requirements.title: Handshake initiator (outbound)
requirements.spec: Implement the client side of the handshake: send name + flags, receive challenge, compute response, send response, receive status.
acceptance: Sends node name and distribution flags to remote
Receives challenge, computes MD5(cookie + challenge) response
Test: complete handshake between two local nodes

requirements.title: Handshake responder (inbound)
requirements.spec: Implement the server side: receive name, send challenge, receive response, verify, send status.
acceptance: Receives remote node name and flags
Test: reject connection with wrong cookie

requirements.title: Distribution flags negotiation
requirements.spec: Exchange and negotiate capability flags during handshake.
acceptance: DistributionFlags bitfield with: PUBLISHED, ATOM_CACHE, EXTENDED_REFERENCES, EXTENDED_PIDS, UTF8_ATOMS, BIG_CREATION, MAP_TAG, HANDSHAKE_23
Both sides send their flags, intersection determines connection capabilities
Minimum required flags enforced (reject incompatible nodes)
Test: flag negotiation produces correct intersection

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement TLS — plain TCP with cookie authentication only
SHALL NOT implement distribution flag FRAGMENTS (large message fragmentation) — that's an optimization for later
SHALL implement the OTP 23+ handshake (version 6), not the legacy handshake
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
